_comment: Damodaran FCFF inputs for TSMC (TSM). Amounts in USD millions.
_data_source: Phase 5 zero-revenue remediation 2026-05-23: revenue and price populated from web search (The Motley Fool / TechTimes). Full financial inputs (CapEx, D&A, debt/equity, R&D) still required — scheduled for Session 10 Phase 3 DCF.
story_narrative: TSMC is the AI infrastructure pick-and-shovel. It gets paid on every advanced node chip — GPU, custom ASIC, or otherwise — regardless of which application wins. N2 ramping in 2025, Arizona fabs adding USD supply chain resilience, CoWoS capacity expanding for HBM/GPU stacking. CapEx peaks 2024–2026; as capacity fills, revenue grows into the fixed cost base and FCF expands sharply. The geopolitical premium is real but the moat — sole supplier of leading-edge nodes below 3nm — is essentially unassailable over the investment horizon.
story_revenue_cagr: 18% reflects HPC/AI segment growing >30% yr/yr dragging blended foundry revenue. N2 at premium ASPs, Arizona adds USD exposure. Bear case 12% if AI CapEx cools.
story_target_margin: 42% is current trajectory; overseas fab cost headwind (Arizona ~30% more expensive than Taiwan) caps upside. Damodaran would note TSMC's historical range is 30–45% EBIT margin.
story_growth_years: 10 years: N2 → A16 → next node. Leading-edge nodes have 3–5 year product cycles; 10 years covers 2 full cycles.
story_stable_growth: 3% = global semiconductor long-run growth. TSMC in stable phase grows with the market.
story_stable_margin: 35% is the mature foundry equilibrium — enough pricing power to stay above commodity margins but offshore fabs normalize cost structure.
story_s2c_high: 0.9x reflects foundry's CapEx intensity (~40–50% of revenue). Sales-to-Capital < 1.0 means TSMC must invest nearly as much as revenue it generates.
story_s2c_stable: 1.0x — as legacy fabs fully depreciate and capacity utilization stabilizes, incremental revenue requires less new capital.
story_beta: 0.9 — foundry is less cyclical than fabless or memory; backlogged orders reduce volatility. Geopolitical premium pushes beta above pure defensive.
story_rd_cap: 5-year amortization for process node R&D. Each new node (N2, A16) has a useful IP life of ~5 years before superseded.
story_uncertainty: Medium — moat is clear but geopolitical tail risk (Taiwan Strait) creates asymmetric downside that makes wide MoS prudent. Offshore fab execution risk adds uncertainty.
